body.title: [sprint] Name the defeating question + classify design-stuck vs capability-stuck
body.description:
**Problem (the hard sub-problem):** raw 'stuck' is low-info. The value is naming WHICH question defeated the builder, AND separating two kinds of wall: (a) DESIGN-STUCK — the ticket/spec didn't say → a genuine unsorted question that sorts into the tree (the gold); (b) CAPABILITY-STUCK — the fixed-tier model simply can't, however well-specified → a capability ceiling, NOT a sortable question. At a fixed tier you get both; only design-stuck belongs in the drop-ticket worklist. A drop-ticket stream that conflates them is noise, not a worklist.

**Proposed shape:** a classifier that, given a stuck run's transcript + the ticket + the wall context, produces {defeating_question: str, kind: design_stuck|capability_stuck, confidence}. Rung-2 (drop-ticket) fires ONLY for design_stuck: it files a new ticket whose body IS the named defeating question, sets the current ticket needs-design, and returns. capability_stuck records the ceiling but does NOT pollute the design worklist. The classifier itself runs through the inference proxy (a cheap tier judging the transcript) or a heuristic-first pass — design the interface so the judgment source is swappable.

**Grounding the eval (advisor 2026-07-05 — DON'T self-consistency-trap this):** classification QUALITY must NOT be graded against author-invented fixtures — that proves the author's notion of design-vs-capability, not reality's. Tickets 1+2 GENERATE the real corpus: harvest-mode-to-the-wall produces real stuck-transcripts, already correlated by ticket_id in io_corpus (with role/turn from T-corpus-visibility-gaps). Grade the classifier against a HELD-OUT slice of those real transcripts, sealed via eval_slice.py (built 2026-07-05), with labels from a HUMAN pass on the real sample — the reality-uncoupled eval. Hand-authored fixtures are allowed ONLY to prove the routing PLUMBING (design_stuck→drop, capability_stuck→no-drop), never classification quality. SEQUENCING: this ticket is gated on 1+2 shipping AND a harvested stuck-slice being sealed — the gate field points at ticket 2, but do not start until a sealed slice exists.

**Affected files:** new unseen_university/devices/inference/stuck_classifier.py (OOP), the rung-2 hook in stuck_ladder.py, ticket_store/cc_queue for the drop-ticket write, eval_slice.py + io_corpus/corpus_verdict for the sealed real-transcript eval.

**Design rules:** inference-proxy-only for any LLM judgment; OOP-first; docs-live-in-code; the drop-ticket write goes through cc_queue.py (canonical writer); log the classification; grade quality only against a sealed eval_slice, never unsealed corpus reads (spend the asymmetry visibly).

**Scope boundary:** classify + drop-ticket-on-design-stuck + the sealed-slice quality eval. NOT the resume loop (next ticket).

**Validation:** the routing PLUMBING — a design-stuck input yields a needs-design drop-ticket whose body names the question; a capability-stuck input yields NO design drop-ticket. Classification QUALITY — the classifier's kind-labels agree with the human labels on the sealed harvested slice above a stated threshold, measured through eval_slice reads (budgeted, logged).

**Observable via:** log.info('StuckClassifier: ticket=%s kind=%s q=%r') per classification; a new needs-design ticket appears in the store for design_stuck; an eval_slice read-log entry per quality-eval consultation.

**Test plan:** (a) PLUMBING PROOF NODE (hermetic, stubbed judgment source, hand fixtures OK here): the design-stuck fixture produces a needs-design drop-ticket AND the capability-stuck fixture does NOT — a naive classifier that drops for BOTH fails the capability-stuck assert. red→green. (b) QUALITY: graded against the sealed harvested slice with human labels — this is a MEASUREMENT, not a red→green proof; report the agreement rate. If 1+2 have not yet produced a sealable slice at sprint time, ship the plumbing proven and the quality eval shipped-unproven naming the lever (sealed harvested stuck-slice not yet available).

**audit-emphasis:** needs-deep-smell (L, fuzzy classifier, HIGH design risk).

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
